questions.question: Why did Lushkoff lie about his profession?
questions.answer: He believed people would not help him if he told the truth.

questions.question: How did Sergei recognise Lushkoff’s lie?
questions.answer: He had seen him earlier telling a different story.

questions.question: Why did Sergei become angry with Lushkoff?
questions.answer: Because Lushkoff was cheating people by lying.

questions.question: What did Sergei suggest Lushkoff should do instead of begging?
questions.answer: He suggested that Lushkoff should work honestly.

questions.question: Why did Lushkoff agree to chop wood?
questions.answer: He agreed out of pride and shame after being exposed.

questions.question: Why was Lushkoff unable to chop wood properly?
questions.answer: He was weak and affected by alcohol.

questions.question: How did Sergei feel when he watched Lushkoff trying to chop wood?
questions.answer: He felt sorry and ashamed.

questions.question: What kind of tasks did Lushkoff do for Sergei later?
questions.answer: He shoveled snow, arranged wood, and beat rugs.

questions.question: How did Sergei reward Lushkoff for his work?
questions.answer: He gave him small amounts of money and clothes.

questions.question: What job did Sergei help Lushkoff get later?
questions.answer: He helped him get copying work.

questions.question: How long did it take for Lushkoff’s life to change?
questions.answer: About two years.

questions.question: What position did Lushkoff hold after reforming?
questions.answer: He became a notary.

questions.question: How much salary did Lushkoff earn?
questions.answer: Thirty-five roubles a month.

questions.question: Why did Sergei feel proud when he met Lushkoff again?
questions.answer: He believed his advice had reformed him.

questions.question: What did Lushkoff reveal about Olga?
questions.answer: She had actually chopped the wood for him.

questions.question: Why did Olga secretly chop the wood?
questions.answer: Because she felt pity for Lushkoff.

questions.question: What effect did Olga’s scolding have on Lushkoff?
questions.answer: It made him feel ashamed and change his ways.

questions.question: Why did Lushkoff stop drinking?
questions.answer: He was moved by Olga’s kindness.

questions.question: What quality of Olga impressed Lushkoff the most?
questions.answer: Her compassion and kindness.

questions.question: What message does the story give about kindness?
questions.answer: Kindness can bring positive change in people.

questions.question: What lesson does Sergei try to teach Lushkoff?
questions.answer: The dignity of honest work.

questions.question: Why is the story called 'The Beggar'?
questions.answer: Because it focuses on the life and transformation of a beggar.

questions.question: What change happened in Lushkoff’s character?
questions.answer: He became responsible and hardworking.

questions.question: What social issue is shown in the story?
questions.answer: Poverty and begging.

questions.question: What is the moral of the story?
questions.answer: Compassion and honest work can transform a person's life.
